content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcel PRACrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95911-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 1,2009
Lois Henry
Board of Directors
Lompico County Water District
11255 Lompico Road
Felton, CA 95018
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09.256
Dear Ms. Henry:
This letter is in response to your request for clarification regarding the Heath
Advice Letter, No. I-09-167, an advice request submitted on behali of Mthael Eggleston,
the District Manager of the Lompico County Water District (the "District"), regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 You w-ere one of
the board members about whom the advice was sought.
In that letter, we analyzed your potential conflict of interest in a water main
decision and found that the only reasonably foreseeable financial effect on your economic
interests was the possible financial effect on your personal finances.2 We siated:
"If the decision will affect the Board Members' personal finances by $250
or more over any l2-month period, it will be deemed material under the
commission's regulations and will have a conflict of interest with respect
to that decision. (Regulations 18704 and 18705.5.) It is unclear how
' The Politicul Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulation-s of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through l8gg7 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
2 In the letter we concluded you did not have an economic interest in the Lake Boulevard Road
Association, of which you are a contributing member. The letter also found that a material financial effect
on your other economic interest, your interest in real property, was not foreseeable. We stated ..[w]hile
repair of the boulevard and construction of the bridge would appear to affect the Board Members,
neighborhoods, including traffic, the decision confronting the District, where to place the new water main,
would not appear to have such an etfect. Our conclusion would be different if the official was called on to
participate in a decision as to the repair of the road or building of the bridge. However, we understand this
to be a project of the Association, and not the District. Thus, it does not appear that the presumption of no
materiality would be rebutted."


File No. A-09-256
Page No. 2
much Board Member Herny contributed to the Association and how much
she may get in return if the Association is paid by the District to attach the
water main to the Association's bridge. However, if the placement of the
water main will result in payments to the Association such that the ioard
Member would recetve $250 or more, the effect on her personal finances
would be material.', [Emphasis added.]
We concluded:
"Board Member Lois Henry is disqualified from voting on the repair and
restoration of the water main if it will be reasonably foreseeableihat there
will be a materialfinanciar effect on her property ir on her personal
finances." [Emphasis added.]
In your November 23,2009 communication, you clarified that the Association
was not currently seeking reimbursement or any payments from the District. However,
whether the financial consequences of a govemmental decision are foreseeable and
material depends on the specific facts surrounding the decision. A financial effect need
not be a certainty to be considered reasonably foriseeable, but it must be more than a
mere possibility. Based on the facts you have provided, we cannot conclude that the
financial effect on your personal finances is or is not reasonable foreseeable. This is a
factual determination that must be made by you.
However, in your November 25,zoog,communication, you stated that even
assuming for argument's sake that at some point in the future thlre were payments to the
Association, and the Association determined the payments would be returned to
contributing members, you would not accept any money in connection with any contract
between the Association and the District, oi from any litigation between the Association
and District, related to the water main breakage on Lake ioulevard.
We have previously advised:
"[I]f a public 9fi9i{ completely and unequivocally renounces any intent
to avail himself of the material financial benefit giving rise to u .onfli.t of
interest, he may participate in the decision. (Neisom,supra; Libow
Advice Letter, No. I-91-461.) In the Newsom letter, we cbncruded that an
official who owned a 'clean air vehicle' could introduce and vote on the
legislation to allow 'clean air vehicles, to park for free provided he
completely and unequivocaily renounced any right to avail himself of
resulting free parking privileges.
***
"[n that letter, it was reasonably foreseeable that the decision would have
resulted in a material financial effect on the official's personal finances.


File No. A-09-256
Page No. 3
(Newsom, supra; regulation 18705.5.) Therefore, the official had the
ability to prevent the financial effect from ultimately occurring by
continuing to pay for parking." (Jacobs Advice Letier, No. e-ol-tzo.)
For example, in the Barisone Advice Letter, No. 4-92-632, we considered
whether a councilmember could participate in city council decisions pertaining to the
factory outlet center proposal and city litigation arising out of that proposat. ffre
councilmember was an active member of an organizationthat opposed the proposed
development of the outlet, had contributed to the organization, and voluntarily served as a
member of the organization's steering committee. We stated:
"However, if it is reasonably foreseeable that Ms. Mathews will recover
attomey's fees in the amount of $250 or more if Friends files an action and
ultimately prevails in the lawsuit, then Ms. Mathews would be required to
disqualify herself from participating in any decision which wouldiave a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on Friends.
*x*
"If Ms. Mathews forfeits any right to recover attorney's fees if Friends
prevails in the lawsuit, she does not have a conflict of interest under the
Act. If she is not reimbursed for her original contribution and does not
receive any money from attorney's fees, she has no discemable financial
interest in the result of the lawsuit.,,
Thus, based on your renunciation of any financial benefit from any contract
between the Association and the District, or from any litigation between tlhe Association
and District related to the water main breakage on Lake Boulevard, a personal financial
effect on you is not foreseeable.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By:
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
Jwwjgl

sections.conclusion: Based on your renunciation of any financial benefit from any contract between the Association and the District, or from any litigation between the Association and District related to the water main breakage on Lake Boulevard, a personal financial effect on you is not foreseeable.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a board member with a potential financial interest in an association that may receive payments from a water district decision is disqualified from voting, particularly in light of the board member's renunciation of any financial benefits from such payments.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that because the board member completely and unequivocally renounced any intent to receive financial benefits from Association payments or litigation with the District, she is not disqualified from voting, as a material financial effect on her is no longer reasonably foreseeable.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Response to prior advice letter (I-09-167). No traditional Q&A section structure, but contains clear question in body of letter and explicit conclusion paragraph. Document discusses renunciation doctrine with multiple precedent cases cited (Newsom, Jacobs, Barisone). Page 1 contains reference to prior letter analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a board member with a potential financial interest in an association that may receive payments from a water district decision is disqualified from voting, particularly in light of the board member's renunciation of any financial benefits from such payments.

CONCLUSION: Based on your renunciation of any financial benefit from any contract between the Association and the District, or from any litigation between the Association and District related to the water main breakage on Lake Boulevard, a personal financial effect on you is not foreseeable.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses a follow-up question regarding Board Member Lois Henry's potential conflict of interest in a water district decision. The FPPC concludes that Henry's explicit renunciation of any financial benefits from the Association eliminates the conflict of interest that would otherwise require her disqualification.
